MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Apr-27-2026 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Sep-29-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs Request For Production Of Documents, Set One
Off Calendar Aug-15-2025 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF CASEY SULLIVAN's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs Request For Production Of Documents, Set One.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff's motion to compel further responses is denied.

The matter was initially on for hearing on May 7, 2025. The court sanctioned plaintiff for failing to meet and confer. The court exercised its discretion to permit the parties to meet and confer about the discovery disputes raised. The only remaining disputes are (1) RFP No. 31; and (2) whether defendant should identify what bates ranges of the production match what responses pursuant to CCP 2031.280. The court denies relief. It concludes that RFP No. 31 is disproportionate to the needs of the case. As to CCP 2031.280, plaintiff did not identify this as a basis for its motion.

Order submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

PLAINTIFF CASEY SULLIVAN'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFFS REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, SET ONE
Off Calendar May-07-2025 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs Request For Production Of Documents, Set One
Off Calendar May-07-2025 Continued To Jul-16-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 301
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
Michael Amir Jahangani, Esq., QUILL & ARROW, LLP (310) 933-4271 mjahangani@quillarrowlaw.com Attorneys for Plaintiff, CASEY SULLIVAN
KEVIN J. TULLY, ESQ., LAW OFFICE OF KEVIN J. TULLY (650) 377-0708 kevin@tullylaw.net Attorney for Defendant FORD MOTOR COMPANY

Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF CASEY SULLIVAN'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs Request For Production Of Documents, Set One

Plaintiff Casey Sullivan's motion to compel further responses to Sullivan's request for production of documents, set one, from Ford Motor Company is granted in part continued on the court's own motion to July 16, 2025.

Ford has offered to produce documents identified in Exhibit A to Sullivan's meet and confer letter of December 27 on condition that it resolves all discovery requests in this set of RFPs. Sullivan responded to Ford's offer with a boilerplate meet and confer letter that did not address the offer at all or meaningfully attempt to narrow the dispute. Sullivan did not accept Ford's offer to telephonically meet and confer.

Moreover, Sullivan does not explain in the moving papers to this court why Ford's offer of production was inadequate. A moving party seeking to compel further responses to a request for production of documents has the burden of setting forth specific facts showing good cause to justify the discovery sought by the demand. (CCP 2031.310(b)(1).)

The court orders Sullivan to meet and confer telephonically or in person concerning the compromise Ford offered in its letter of January 15, 2024. The court is hopeful that the parties will resolve their outstanding discovery disputes. The motion is continued to July 16 unless it is withdrawn by Sullivan in the interim. Sullivan shall file a supplemental brief no later than nine court days in advance of the continued hearing date concerning what issues are still in dispute. Sullivan must also file an updated separate statement that shows specific facts and good cause to order production of any documents that remain in dispute. Boilerplate assertions of relevance will not suffice. Ford may respond by supplemental brief and updated separate statement by no later than five court days in advance of the hearing.

CCP 2031.300(c) provides that "the court shall impose a monetary sanction . . . against any party, person, or attorney who unsuccessfully makes or opposes a motion to compel a response to a demand for inspection . . . unless it finds that the one subject to the sanction acted with substantial justification or that other circumstances make the imposition of the sanction unjust." The failure to meet and confer is a misuse of the discovery process and burdens the court with reviewing disputes that the parties could resolve through good-faith efforts. (CCP 2023.010(i).) The court finds that Sullivan acted without substantial justification in failing to meet and confer and orders Sullivan to pay sanctions of $500 to the San Francisco Superior Court within 10 court days of entry of this order.

Counsel for movant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)
